meaning: adj.应负责的,有责任的,可解释的
examples: Someone must be held accountable for the killings.
Politicians are ultimately accountable to the voters.
Public officials can finally be held accountable for their actions.
He is also known for holding politicians and public officials accountable in his "No Talking Points" segment.
Any person complicit in the violation of that trust must be held accountable for their actions.
